name: Under Armour, Inc.

profile.description: Under Armour, Inc. develops, markets, and distributes performance apparel, footwear, and accessories. Founder Kevin Plank returned as CEO in 2024 to lead a turnaround. Revenue ~$5.2B annually.

innovation.label: WEAK
innovation.analysis: Track Record (1/3): Brand turnaround underway but not yet proven. AI Deployment (0/2): No notable AI. Market Share (1/2): #3 U.S. performance apparel behind Nike and Adidas. Product Pipeline (0/2): No transformative products. R&D Intensity (1/1): Product development investment.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Athletic performance innovation purpose. Community Impact (1/2): UA Foundation programs. Employee Retention (0/2): CEO changes and restructuring. Insider Buying (1/2): Plank bought stock on return.

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored. D/E 137.14% (0.71). ROE -30% (0.0, 2x). Revenue -0.8% (0.64). Forward P/E 22.95 (0.93). Profit margin -9.98% (0.0). Very weak fundamentals.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Physical apparel manufacturing and distribution. Brand and retail presence provide moats. AI may enhance design/supply chain but doesn't replace products. AI Opportunity (0/1): No AI revenue.

infinite_game.summary: Under Armour under returned founder Kevin Plank is attempting a turnaround. Plank's return shows commitment but trust was damaged.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (0/2): CEO revolving door and restructuring.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Uses Nike as motivation.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Plank returned to lead turnaround.

investment_thesis: Under Armour with founder Kevin Plank back as CEO at $5.56/share and $2.4B market cap — deep value if turnaround succeeds. Brand has global recognition. D/E of 137% is manageable.
thesis_continued: Revenue declining -0.8% with -10% profit margin and -30% ROE. Brand has lost relevance vs Nike/Adidas/Lululemon. Forward P/E of 22.95 is expensive for a money-losing turnaround.
key_catalysts: Plank turnaround driving brand repositioning and margin recovery
International expansion providing growth outside mature U.S. market
key_risks: Revenue declining with negative margins — turnaround timing uncertain
Brand relevance erosion vs Nike, Adidas, Lululemon, and athleisure competitors
Forward P/E of 22.95 is expensive for a company with negative profitability
sources: Under Armour FY2026 Earnings — SEC Filings, Yahoo Finance, 2026
